title: Urochloa leersioides
taxonomy: Urochloa leersioides is classified as a species of grass in the family Poaceae. It belongs to the genus Urochloa, which is a group of grasses native to tropical and subtropical regions. Urochloa leersioides is further classified as a member of the subfamily Panicoideae. The species is also known by its synonym Brachiaria leersioides. Within the genus Urochloa, Urochloa leersioides is a distinct species with its unique characteristics.

intro: Urochloa leersioides, also known as Jaraguá grass, is a perennial grass native to the tropical regions of South America, belonging to the family Poaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, due to its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Urochloa leersioides include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Urochloa leersioides is often used to prepare teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used to treat various health conditions, and it is also used as an ingredient in some traditional remedies for skin and wound care.
